title: Pod Security, Security Contexts, Images, Admission, Secrets, and Multi-Tenancy · Cluster Repair Bay
summary: An incident responder inherits a plausible but defective restricted workload and admission policy; preserve status and events, isolate the first causal fault in pod security, security contexts, images, admission, secrets, and multi-tenancy, and retain a regression check. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in restricted workload and admission policy and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case p…
objectives: Apply Privileged, Baseline, and Restricted Pod Security Standards with enforce, audit, warn, version pinning, exemptions, namespace labels, and rollout evidence.
Set non-root identity, groups, filesystem ownership, read-only root, capabilities, privilege escalation, seccomp, host namespaces, devices, and writable mounts from threat evidence.
Require immutable images, provenance, signatures, vulnerability policy, registry allowlists, architecture, pull credentials, and admission decisions with exceptions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug K8s Pod Security Standards Admission
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug Pod runs as root, is privileged, adds capabilities, mounts hostPath, permits privilege escalation, and bypasses a namespace policy exception. Enforce Pod Security, securityContext, seccomp, admission, exception ownership, and residual shared-kernel risk. Competency probe: Apply Privileged, Baseline, and Restricted Pod Security Standards with enforce, audit, warn, version pinning, exemptions, namespace labels, and rollout evidence.

Inspect the K8s Pod Security Standards Admission trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Inspect the failure record · Debug K8s Pod Security Standards Admission.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for apply privileged, baseline, and restricted pod security standards with enforce, audit, warn, version pinning, exemptions, namespace labels, and rollout evidence. For Inspect the failure record · Debug K8s Pod Security Standards Admission, keep K8s Pod Security Standards Admission connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug K8s Multitenancy Isolation Defense
steps.instruction:
Append the Kubernetes evidence item headed "  # Evidence: k8s-multitenancy-isolation-defense-kuch5c". Tailor its restricted-workload-policy.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic API or manifest review command, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run kubectl, Helm, kubeadm, and cluster commands later in an authorized disposable transfer environment.

Evidence target: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug K8s Multitenancy Isolation Defense.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns K8s Multitenancy Isolation Defense into observable performance evidence. For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug K8s Multitenancy Isolation Defense, keep K8s Multitenancy Isolation Defense connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: k8s-multitenancy-isolation-defense-kuch5c
  - competency: k8s-multitenancy-isolation-defense
    artifact: restricted-workload-policy.yaml
    decision: multitenancy isolation defense
    environment: kubeadm recovery cluster restored onto replacement nodes
    fault-injection: withdraw the selected image digest after one node pulls it
    retained-proof: accessible incident timeline with causal regression evidence
    case-key: kuch5c
    change: |
      pod-security: restricted-v1.36
      runAsNonRoot: true
      allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
      capabilities-drop: ALL
      seccompProfile: RuntimeDefault
      readOnlyRootFilesystem: true
      image: digest-and-signature-required
      admission: fail-closed-with-breakglass
    verify: kubectl auth can-i create pods -n intake && kubectl apply --server-side --dry-run=server -f restricted.yaml
    failure: Reject removed PodSecurityPolicy, namespace-only multi-tenancy, mutable images, privileged host access, broad secrets, or unavailable admission without a plan.
    repair: Enforce versioned Pod Security, minimize container and API authority, isolate network and storage, verify supply chain, and test break-glass recovery.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

steps.title: Publish the incident record · Debug K8s Multitenancy Isolation Defense
steps.instruction:
Write the debug evidence note for K8s Multitenancy Isolation Defense: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A debug Pod runs as root, is privileged, adds capabilities, mounts hostPath, permits privilege escalation, and bypasses a namespace policy exception. Enforce Pod Security, securityContext, seccomp, admission, exception ownership, and residual shared-kernel risk. Competency probe: Combine namespaces, RBAC, quotas, network policy, storage classes, scheduling isolation, Pod security, admission, audit, and dedicated clusters according to trust.

Evidence target: Publish the incident record · Debug K8s Multitenancy Isolation Defense.
steps.why: Retrieving K8s Multitenancy Isolation Defense through the concrete restricted workload and admission policy case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Publish the incident record · Debug K8s Multitenancy Isolation Defense, keep K8s Multitenancy Isolation Defense connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Combine namespaces, RBAC, quotas, network policy, storage classes, scheduling isolation, Pod security, admission, audit, and dedicated clusters according to trust.

hints: For Publish the incident record · Debug K8s Multitenancy Isolation Defense, name the context, namespace, API identity, desired state, observed status, controller, policy, and failure-domain boundary, K8s Multitenancy Isolation Defense precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Publish the incident record · Debug K8s Multitenancy Isolation Defense, trace this concrete input and predict admission, defaulting, scheduling, reconciliation, status, traffic, storage, policy, disruption, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: A debug Pod runs as root, is privileged, adds capabilities, mounts hostPath, permits privilege escalation, and bypasses a namespace policy exception. Enforce Pod Security, securityContext, seccomp, admission, exception ownership, and residual shared-kernel risk. Competency probe: Combine namespaces, RBAC, quotas, network policy, storage classes, scheduling isolation, Pod security, admission, audit, and dedicated clusters according to trust.

For Publish the incident record · Debug K8s Multitenancy Isolation Defense, challenge the documented misconception with object, condition, event, policy, failure, repair, rollback, and transfer evidence: Namespaces alone create a complete hard multi-tenant security boundary.
